title: Zebkit Component Manifest
description: Hand-authored guidance layer of a component's contract: the slot vocabulary and semantics, usage guidance, and canonical examples. It never restates what code already declares — attributes, tokens, events, and variants stay derived from the Custom Elements Manifest, token modules, and variant registry. build:context merges this manifest with those derived sources into the per-component agent context; a build step emits each component's runtime slot-name list from it so unknown-slot warnings share one source of truth. lint:components keeps the round trip honest.

tag.description: The custom element tag. Must have a declaration in custom-elements.json (the lint flags a manifest for a component that does not exist).

purpose.description: One or two sentences: what the component is for. The first thing an agent reads when choosing a component.

nativeElement.description: The native element at the component's core (GRAMMAR.md §8), e.g. 'input', 'button'. Stated structurally so tooling need not parse it out of prose.

useWhen.description: Situations this component is the right choice for, e.g. 'Input is prose-length or multi-line'.

case.description: The situation where this component is the wrong choice.

instead.description: What to reach for instead. When it starts with 'zbk-' the lint checks it is a real component tag.

notWhen.description: Situations where another pattern is correct. The component-selection half of the contract.

guidance.description: Component-level usage rules a design tool or AI must respect, e.g. 'A placeholder is never a substitute for label content.'

slots.description: The component's slot contract, keyed by slot name ('default' = unnamed children). The manifest is the source of truth: build:context renders the Slots section from here (no @slot JSDoc), the generated runtime name list drives unknown-slot warnings, and the lint diffs declared names against the slot names the component source actually delivers (slotted()/renderIcon/affix call sites) in both directions. The enum above IS the shared vocabulary from GRAMMAR.md §7 — adding a name here is amending the spec.

does.description: What happens, noting when it is native behavior the component preserves rather than implements.

keyboard.description: The keyboard interaction contract (GRAMMAR.md §8). Omit only when the component adds nothing beyond ordinary native text editing.

examples.description: Canonical markup, rendered into docs and agent context. The lint parses each html string and validates it: tags exist in custom-elements.json, attributes exist on the component, slot attributes name declared slots, variant values are registered. Examples are executable documentation — a renamed attribute breaks the build instead of silently lying to every reader.

variantTiers.description: Usage tier per shipped variant, keyed by variant name (lint-checked against the variant registry). Only list variants that differ from the 'recommended' default. Advisory metadata for tooling — never changes generated CSS, mirroring utility-family tiers.

related.description: Components commonly considered alongside or composed with this one. Each must be a real component tag.

description.description: What content belongs here and what the component does with it.

required.description: True when the component computes an empty accessible name without this slot's content (unless an aria-* attribute supplies the name). Feeds the dev-mode accessible-name warning — one declaration, two renderings.

presentational.description: True when the slot's rendered container is aria-hidden. Information presentational content carries must also live in the accessible name or description (GRAMMAR.md §7).

tokens.description: Component tokens that govern slotted content. Each value must be a key in the component's token module (lint-checked).

size.description: Token key sizing the slotted content, e.g. 'icon-size', 'indicator-size'.

color.description: Token key coloring the slotted content, e.g. 'affix-ink', 'indicator-color'.

positions.description: Placements the slot honors via data-position (icon slots). Omit for slots with a fixed position.

guidance.description: Slot-specific usage rules, e.g. the presentational-content rule spelled out for this slot.

html.description: The authored markup as a consumer would write it (pre-upgrade light DOM). Parsed and validated by the lint.

tier.description: Omit = recommended. 'discouraged' examples exist to show the anti-pattern by name; pair with 'why'.

why.description: Why this example is tiered as it is. Required in spirit for anything below recommended — say what breaks.
